parameters.text: =✅ Automate a multi-step process with HTTP / REST API and PostgreSQL end  — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO job_log (jobId, type, payload, logged_at)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3, NOW());

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 12 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Map to Canonical Schema

### Map to Canonical Schema

Shapes the item: writes `jobId`, `kind`, `payload`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Route by Event Kind

### Route by Event Kind

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: job.start, job.done + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Handle 'job.start' → PostgreSQL · ②→ Handle 'job.done' → Forward API · ③→ Log Unrecognized Kind

### Handle 'job.start' → PostgreSQL

Runs a parameterized `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT` upsert — parameters are bound via `queryReplacement`, never string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Consolidate Handling · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Handle 'job.done' → Forward API

POST https://api.example.com/v1/jobs — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Consolidate Handling · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Log Unrecognized Kind

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Consolidate Handling · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Consolidate Handling

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per job (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Appends a new row to 'Job Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Summarize Run Stats · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Job Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Telegram · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#automation`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Postgres connection

# Automate a multi-step process with HTTP / REST API and PostgreSQL end to end

**Pattern:** Workflow Automation · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Automate a multi-step process with HTTP / REST API and PostgreSQL end to end. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual integrations is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Map to Canonical Schema → Route by Event Kind → Handle 'job.start' → PostgreSQL → Consolidate Handling → Fingerprint for Idempotency → Drop Already-Processed Items → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to workflow automation without manual effort
- Anyone automating integrations in productivity
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Standardizes the process every time
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Postgres connection; Slack OAuth2; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The optional HMAC check runs before any business logic touches the payload — bad signatures are rejected at the door rather than after work has already been done with untrusted data. Acking fast (HTTP 202) and doing the real work after the response is what stops the sender's webhook delivery from timing out and re-delivering the same event.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Turn on signature verification and trace what a tampered payload does at the 'Signature Valid?' gate.
2. Add a new event kind to the switch and give it its own handler.
3. Remove the fast-ack step and observe how response time changes under a slow downstream write.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your integrations process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
